test.name: Irish famine context for 1840s Pennsylvania immigration research
test.description: Provide historical context for Irish immigration to Pennsylvania coal country in the 1840s-1850s. Tests whether the skill consults wiki_search for FamilySearch-Wiki content and produces context tied to the research-relevant attributes (time, place, demographic) rather than generic 19th-century history.

input.user_message: What historical context should I know about Irish immigration to Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania in the 1840s?

judge_context: Should mention the Great Famine (1845-1852) as the primary driver of Irish emigration in this period — not just 'Irish came to America in the 1800s' generic context
Should note that Schuylkill County's anthracite coal industry was a destination for Irish laborers, particularly through Philadelphia (the main entry port for Pennsylvania-bound Irish)

Should surface at least two research-actionable consequences: (a) specific record classes (Castle Garden / Famine emigration manifests, Catholic parish baptismal records since most Famine-era Irish were Catholic), (b) FAN-research opportunities (Irish communities tended to migrate in extended-family clusters; neighbors and witnesses in Schuylkill records often share origin parishes)

When recommending passenger list / arrival manifest research, should reflect that U.S. customs passenger lists from 1820 onward name every individual aboard — including infants and young children — in their own right (name, age, sex, occupation, country of origin; more detail after 1891). A child who emigrated young is listed by name, not folded into a parent's entry, so the whole manifest should be examined for the complete family group. Must NOT claim that a young child was 'rarely listed separately' or 'traveled under a parent's entry' — that is factually wrong for U.S. arrivals from 1820 on.
